subject: GRB030913 (=H2849): TAROT Observations
body:
This is a multi-part message in MIME format. ------=_NextPart_000_0090_01C37D53.597BA9A0 Content-Type: text/plain; charset="us-ascii" Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit Alain Klotz, Michel Boer (CESR/CNRS/UPS) and Jean-Luc Atteia (LAT/OMP/UPS), on behalf of the TAROT team report: Following the GCN notices about GRB 030913, and GCNC #2383, The automated TAROT telescope took several images of the possible location of the source of GRB 030913. The images were taken between 20:33 and 20:44 UT on Sept., 13, 2003. No new object is present on the 2 deg. x 2deg. image centered on the GCN notice positon. The Moon is relatively close to the source position (72deg.), hence the limit we could obtain on the possible source magnitude is only R = 17.2 This message is citable. ------=_NextPart_000_0090_01C37D53.597BA9A0 Content-Type: text/html; charset="us-ascii" Content-Transfer-Encoding: quoted-printable

Alain = Klotz, Michel Boer (CESR/CNRS/UPS) and Jean-Luc Atteia (LAT/OMP/UPS), on behalf of the = TAROT team report:

 

Following the GCN notices about GRB 030913, and GCNC #2383, The automated TAROT telescope = took several images of the possible location of the source of GRB 030913. The = images were taken between 20:33 and 20:44 = UT on Sept., 13, 2003. No new object is present on the 2 deg. x 2deg. image = centered on the GCN notice positon. The Moon is relatively close to the source = position (72deg.), hence the limit we could obtain on the possible source = magnitude is only R =3D 17.2

 

 

This message is = citable.

 

 

------=_NextPart_000_0090_01C37D53.597BA9A0--
